---
title: "batch1-4_preQC"
output: html_document
date: "2026-01-28"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```



```{r}
## load singleCellTK
library(singleCellTK, lib="/restricted/projectnb/camplab/home/jiaqi/R/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-library/4.4")
```

Read in the combined sce object for batch1-4

```{r}

sce_all <-readRDS("/restricted/projectnb/cteseq/data/CTE_Single_Cell/processed snRNA-seq batches/sce_1234_QC_clustered.rds")

```


```{r}
table(sce_all$batch)
```


```{r}
table(sce_all$sample)
```


```{r}
print(sce_all)
```



UMAP - already ran

```{r}
# UMAP builds a k-NN graph in that PC space and then lays the cells out in 2D.
# sce_all <- runSeuratUMAP(inSCE = sce_all, useReduction = "pca", reducedDimName = "seuratUMAP", dims = 15)

```


Plot PCA result by clusters

```{r}
# sce_all <- runSeuratFindClusters(inSCE = sce_all, useAssay = "seuratScaledData", useReduction = "pca", dims = 15, algorithm = "louvain")
plotSeuratReduction(sce_all, "pca", showLegend = TRUE)

```


plot UMAP result by clusters

```{r}
plotSeuratReduction(sce_all, "umap", showLegend = TRUE)
```



plot sce Umap by batch
```{r}
library(scater)
plotReducedDim(sce_all, dimred = "seuratUMAP", colour_by = "batch", point_size = 0.8)

```





#plot sce UMAP by doublets
```{r}
library(scater)
plotReducedDim(sce_all, dimred = "seuratUMAP", colour_by = "scDblFinder_doublet_score", point_size = 1)+
  scale_colour_gradientn(
  colours = c("grey80", "salmon", "red2"),  # tweak if you want stronger contrast,
  name="Doublet score"
)

```


UMAP by Contimanation

```{r}
#grep("scDblFinder", colnames(colData(sce)), value = TRUE)

plotReducedDim(sce_all, dimred = "seuratUMAP", colour_by = "decontX_contamination")->p
p + scale_colour_gradientn(
  colours = c("grey80", "mediumpurple1", "blue2"),  # tweak if you want stronger contrast
  name    = "Ambient\nContamination Score"
)

```



# HEATMAP by cluster 

```{r}
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scuttle)        
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(viridis)

cluster_col   <- "Seurat_louvain_Resolution0.8"
cluster_order <- as.character(0:35)

# marker genes (same as the paper)
markers <- c(
  "SLC17A7","RALYL","GAD1","GAD2","COL9A1","LHFPL3",
  "CD69","IL7R","MBP","PLP1","PTPRC","CX3CR1",
  "CLDN5","NOSTRIN","PDGFRB","LAMA2","GFAP","ALDH1L1"
)
markers <- intersect(markers, rownames(sce_all))   # keep only genes that exist


# subset to chosen clusters, "Seurat_louvain_Resolution0.8" is stored in sce, coldata
sce_sub_heat <- sce_all[, sce_all[[cluster_col]] %in% cluster_order] 

## ---- use seuratNormData as the "counts" assay ----
sce_tmp <- sce_sub_heat
assay(sce_tmp, "counts") <- assay(sce_tmp, "seuratNormData")

# 1) average expression per cluster

avg <- aggregateAcrossCells(
  sce_tmp,
  ids        = sce_tmp[[cluster_col]], #Groups cells by their cluster label
  statistics = "mean" #Computes the mean of the (normalized) expression per gene within each cluster.
)

# aggregated assay is named "mean"
avg_mat <- assay(avg, "counts")[markers, , drop = FALSE] #Subsets to the markers rows and all cluster columns.

# reorder columns 
avg_mat <- avg_mat[, cluster_order, drop = FALSE]

# 2) row-wise scaling (like "Scaled expression" in the paper)


# Each gene (row) has been z-scored across clusters.
scaled_mat <- t(scale(t(as.matrix(avg_mat))))

# 3) heatmap: markers (rows) × clusters (cols)
Heatmap(
  scaled_mat,
  col               = viridis::mako(50, direction = -1),
  cluster_rows      = FALSE,
  cluster_columns   = FALSE,       
  row_order         = markers,
  rect_gp          = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 0.3),  
  column_order      = cluster_order,
  row_names_side    = "left",
  column_names_side = "top",
  column_names_rot  = 45,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(
    title      = "Scaled expression",
    direction  = "horizontal"
  )
)

```




# DOT PLOT by cluster


```{r}
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(viridis)

cluster_col   <- "Seurat_louvain_Resolution0.8"
cluster_order <- as.character(0:35)  

markers <- c(
  "SLC17A7","RALYL","GAD1","GAD2","COL9A1","LHFPL3",
  "CD69","IL7R","MBP","PLP1","PTPRC","CX3CR1",
  "CLDN5","NOSTRIN","PDGFRB","LAMA2","GFAP","ALDH1L1"
)
markers <- intersect(markers, rownames(sce_all))

## 1) Subset to chosen clusters
sce_sub <- sce_all[, sce_all[[cluster_col]] %in% cluster_order]

## 2) Pull expression matrix (log-normalized)
# Extracts the "seuratNormData" assay (usually log-normalized counts from Seurat).
# Subsets to marker genes only.

expr <- assay(sce_sub, "seuratNormData")[markers, , drop = FALSE]

library(dplyr)
## 3) Long format with cluster labels
df_long <- as.matrix(expr) |>
  t() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(cluster = sce_sub[[cluster_col]]) |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = all_of(markers),
    names_to  = "gene",
    values_to = "expr"
  )

df_long$cluster <- factor(df_long$cluster, levels = cluster_order)
df_long$gene    <- factor(df_long$gene,    levels = rev(markers))  # top-to-bottom like heatmap

## 4) Summarise per gene × cluster
dot_df <- df_long |>
  group_by(cluster, gene) |>
  summarise(
    avg_expr = mean(expr),
    pct_expr = mean(expr > 0),   # fraction of cells expressing
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  group_by(gene) |>
  mutate(
    scaled_expr = (avg_expr - mean(avg_expr)) / sd(avg_expr)  # row-wise scaling
  ) |>
  ungroup()

library(viridis)

ggplot(dot_df, aes(x = cluster, y = gene)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = pct_expr, colour = scaled_expr)) +
  scale_size(range = c(1, 6), name = "Fraction expressing") +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(
    option    = "viridis",   # matches the attached plot
    direction = 1,
    name      = "Avg scaled\nexpression"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Cluster", y = "Marker gene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid  = element_blank()
  )



```




# RE-organized Major Cell Types Heatmap
```{r}
library(SingleCellExperiment)
library(scuttle)       
library(ComplexHeatmap)
library(viridis)

cluster_col   <- "Seurat_louvain_Resolution0.8"
cluster_ids   <- as.character(0:35)

# marker genes (same as the paper)
markers <- c(
  "SLC17A7","RALYL","GAD1","GAD2","COL9A1","LHFPL3",
  "CD69","IL7R","MBP","PLP1","PTPRC","CX3CR1",
  "CLDN5","NOSTRIN","PDGFRB","LAMA2","GFAP","ALDH1L1"
)
markers <- intersect(markers, rownames(sce_all))   # keep only genes that exist

## 1) Define cluster → major cell-type mapping (using your labels)

cluster_to_type <- setNames(rep(NA_character_, length(cluster_ids)), cluster_ids)


cluster_to_type[c("5","8","10","11", "12", "13", "14", "15","16","18","28","29")] <- "Exc"

cluster_to_type[c("6","7","21","24")] <- "Inh"

cluster_to_type[c("4","34","35")] <- "OPCs"

cluster_to_type["31"] <- "T_cells"

cluster_to_type[c("1","0","23","30","32")] <- "Oligo"

cluster_to_type[c("9","20")] <- "Micro"

cluster_to_type[c("17", "26")] <- "Endo"

cluster_to_type[c("22","25","27")] <- "Mural"

cluster_to_type[c("3","2","33", "19")] <- "Astro"

## 2) Subset to cells that belong to one of these labeled clusters

sce_sub_heat <- sce_all[, sce_all[[cluster_col]] %in% names(cluster_to_type)]

major_type_per_cell <- cluster_to_type[ as.character(sce_sub_heat[[cluster_col]]) ]
keep <- !is.na(major_type_per_cell)

sce_sub_heat       <- sce_sub_heat[, keep]
major_type_per_cell <- major_type_per_cell[keep]

## 3) Use seuratNormData as "counts" for aggregation

sce_tmp <- sce_sub_heat
assay(sce_tmp, "counts") <- assay(sce_tmp, "seuratNormData")

## 4) Average expression per *major cell type* (not per cluster)

avg_type <- aggregateAcrossCells(
  sce_tmp,
  ids        = major_type_per_cell,  # group by major cell type
  statistics = "mean"
)

avg_mat <- assay(avg_type, "counts")[markers, , drop = FALSE]

# Order columns by major cell-type order
type_order <- c("Exc","Inh","OPCs", "T_cells", "Oligo","Micro", "Endo","Mural","Astro")
avg_mat <- avg_mat[, intersect(type_order, colnames(avg_mat)), drop = FALSE]

## 5) Row-wise scaling (z-score per gene across major types)

scaled_mat <- t(scale(t(as.matrix(avg_mat))))

## 6) Heatmap: markers (rows) × major cell types (cols)

Heatmap(
  scaled_mat,
  col               = viridis::mako(50, direction = -1),
  cluster_rows      = FALSE,
  cluster_columns   = FALSE,
  row_order         = markers,
  rect_gp           = gpar(col = "white", lwd = 0.3),
  column_order      = colnames(scaled_mat),   # major types in chosen order
  row_names_side    = "left",
  column_names_side = "top",
  column_names_rot  = 45,
  heatmap_legend_param = list(
    title      = "Scaled expression",
    direction  = "horizontal"
  )
)


```





# RE-organized Major Cell Types DOT PLOT

```{r}

sce_sub <- sce_all[, sce_all[[cluster_col]] %in% cluster_ids]

major_type_per_cell <- cluster_to_type[ as.character(sce_sub[[cluster_col]]) ]
keep <- !is.na(major_type_per_cell)

sce_sub             <- sce_sub[, keep]
major_type_per_cell <- major_type_per_cell[keep]

## 3) Expression matrix (log-normalized)

expr <- assay(sce_sub, "seuratNormData")[markers, , drop = FALSE]

## 4) Long format with major cell-type labels

df_long <- as.matrix(expr) |>
  t() |>
  as.data.frame() |>
  mutate(major_type = major_type_per_cell) |>
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = all_of(markers),
    names_to  = "gene",
    values_to = "expr"
  )

type_order <- c("Exc","Inh","OPCs", "T_cells", "Oligo","Micro","Endo","Mural","Astro")
df_long$major_type <- factor(df_long$major_type, levels = type_order)
df_long$gene       <- factor(df_long$gene, levels = rev(markers))

## 5) Summarise per gene × major cell type

dot_df <- df_long |>
  group_by(major_type, gene) |>
  summarise(
    avg_expr = mean(expr),
    pct_expr = mean(expr > 0),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) |>
  group_by(gene) |>
  mutate(
    scaled_expr = (avg_expr - mean(avg_expr)) / sd(avg_expr)
  ) |>
  ungroup()

## 6) Dot plot: major cell type on x-axis

ggplot(dot_df, aes(x = major_type, y = gene)) +
  geom_point(aes(size = pct_expr, colour = scaled_expr)) +
  scale_size(range = c(1, 6), name = "Fraction expressing") +
  scale_colour_viridis_c(
    option    = "viridis",
    direction = 1,
    name      = "Avg scaled\nexpression"
  ) +
  labs(x = "Major cell type", y = "Marker gene") +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    panel.grid  = element_blank()
  )

```
